documents.author: Christopher A. Kahn
documents.description: Operational constraint and hygiene procedures for the local Claude Code / Cowork transcript cache. Documents the scrubber.py scope boundary (API traffic only, not the keyboard boundary), inventories the four local state paths, records server-side retention verification as of 2026-04-11 (training toggle OFF, 30-day retention), and provides hygiene commands for inspection, retention tuning, manual wipe, and telemetry suppression. Captures the durable glob pattern for PRISM main repo + all worktrees.
documents.title: Transcript Cache Hygiene

documents.author: Christopher A. Kahn
documents.description: Six-phase (A-F) roadmap to extract prism-doc-librarian into a standalone doc-librarian project with a thin PRISM wrapper. Locks Session 25 scope: all 4 manifest types (portable JSON, SHA-256 cryptographic, dependency graph, plugin install), all 4 audit types (drift, IP evidence pack, diff, operation log), both web outputs (dashboard now, static site later), SHA-256 for IP/legal defensibility, full-text index (Lunr) in Phase D with temporal index (git log) in Phase C and vector index (sentence-transformers) deferred to Phase F. Documents 3 unresolved Phase A routing options: new Cowork mount (recommended), temp workspace + archive, or extraction folder inside PRISM. 15-25 hours total across 4-6 sessions. Superseded by V1.1 (same session) to apply project rename doc-librarian -> librarian and skill rename prism-doc-librarian -> prism-librarian.
documents.title: Doc Librarian Buildout Plan

documents.author: Christopher A. Kahn
documents.description: V1.1 of the six-phase (A-F) roadmap to extract the librarian into a standalone project with a thin PRISM wrapper. Supersedes V1.0 same session. Changes from V1.0: project renamed doc-librarian -> librarian; PRISM skill renamed prism-doc-librarian -> prism-librarian (directory skills/prism-doc-librarian/ -> skills/prism-librarian/); Phase A routing decision recorded as resolved (option 1 - new Cowork mount selected); added name collision risk note for Phase F publication. No scope, phase, capability, or timeline changes. Locks Session 25 scope: all 4 manifest types (portable JSON, SHA-256 cryptographic, dependency graph, plugin install), all 4 audit types (drift, IP evidence pack, diff, operation log), both web outputs, SHA-256 for IP/legal defensibility, full-text index in Phase D, temporal index in Phase C, vector index deferred to Phase F. 15-25 hours total across 4-6 sessions.
documents.title: Doc Librarian Buildout Plan

documents.author: Christopher A. Kahn
documents.description: V1.2 — reconciled with actual Phase A deliverables (Sessions 26-27). Phase A overdelivered: pip-installable Python package v0.1.0 with 4 CLI subcommands and 36 pytest cases instead of planned file-only scaffold. PRISM wrapper is 110-line CLI delegation wrapper. Deferred items (schemas, examples) moved to Phase B. Phases B-D updated: standalone scripts replaced by integrated CLI subcommands. Phase A marked complete with commit hashes (7d5fcab librarian, 5389e83 PRISM). Added Cowork sandbox limitation and tracked_dirs gap to risks.
documents.title: Doc Librarian Buildout Plan

registry_meta.last_audit_cowork_note: 2026-04-11 Cowork audit (OODA): 36 registered = 36 on disk, 0 orphans, 0 missing files, 0 pending cross-references. Touch-up applied: appended 6 archived legacy files to naming_violation_details and bumped the naming_violations counter 19 -> 25. All 6 added entries are retained per the transition rule (REGISTRY.yaml lines 11-31) and will migrate on next version bump. Follow-up session (2026-04-11, later): backlog items 5-8 addressed — dashboard V1.1 title suffix removed, field locations_on_disk renamed to total_on_disk with scope comment, 5 previously-empty docs/ subdirs populated with README.md scaffolding (decisions/, legal/, proposals/, reviews/, roadmap/), dashboard V2.0 stale metadata refreshed to match registry counts. README.md files are infrastructure-exempt and not registered individually. Item 8 closed by creating doc-librarian-dashboard-20260411-V2.1.html, a self-contained HTML wrapper around the V2.0 JSX component using React 18 UMD + Babel standalone from unpkg CDN — restores the double-click-to-open property that V1.1 had but V2.0 lost. V2.0.jsx marked superseded; V1.1.html superseded_by repointed to V2.1.html. Totals: 36 -> 37 documents, 10 -> 11 superseded. Items 9 and 10 prepared as an operator-runnable cleanup script (scripts/prism-operator-cleanup-20260411-V1.0.sh) because the Cowork sandbox cannot unlink files. Item 9 targets 24 superseded PRISM files in ~/Downloads/ with per-file repo verification; item 10 targets sandbox-blocked repo artifacts: scripts/*.deleted, docs/.trash/ (6 files), .git/*.lock.abandoned-* (38), and stale .git/objects/**/tmp_obj_* (114). Script is dry-run by default, executes with --execute, scoped with --item 9 or --item 10. Follow-up (2026-04-11, later): new operational constraint doc added — transcript-cache-hygiene-20260411-V1.0.md — captures the scrubber.py scope boundary (API traffic only, not the local transcript cache), inventories the four local state paths, records server-side retention verification (training toggle OFF, 30-day retention), and provides hygiene commands for the ~/.claude/projects/**/*.jsonl plaintext cache. CLAUDE.md gets a single-line pointer under Key Constraints per minimal-footprint policy. Totals: 37 -> 38 documents, 26 -> 27 active. Follow-up (2026-04-11, Session 25): librarian buildout plan V1.0 added — librarian-buildout-plan-20260411-V1.0.md — six-phase roadmap (A-F) to extract prism-doc-librarian into a standalone doc-librarian project with thin PRISM wrapper. Locks scope: all 4 manifest types (portable JSON, SHA-256, dependency graph, plugin install), all 4 audit types (drift, IP evidence, diff, op log), both web outputs, SHA-256 for IP/legal defensibility, Lunr full-text index Phase D, temporal index Phase C, vector index deferred to Phase F. Phase A execution deferred to Session 26 pending routing decision (new Cowork mount recommended). Totals: 38 -> 39 documents, 27 -> 28 active. Follow-up (2026-04-11, Session 25, late): project rename applied — standalone project doc-librarian -> librarian, PRISM wrapper skill prism-doc-librarian -> prism-librarian. Directory skills/prism-doc-librarian/ renamed via atomic os.rename to skills/prism-librarian/. SKILL.md frontmatter name field updated. CLAUDE.md Librarian Commits section (line 170) updated. Plan doc bumped V1.0 -> V1.1 with rename applied and Phase A routing decision recorded as resolved (option 1). V1.0 retained on disk, marked superseded_by V1.1. Setup script scripts/setup/prism-doc-setup.sh path references updated in place (infrastructure edit, non-registered). REGISTRY header comment and project_config comment updated to reflect new skill name. Deferred follow-ups (flagged but not executed this commit): toolchain setup guide prism-toolchain-setup-guide-20260411.md needs V1.0 -> V1.1 bump pairing the rename fix with its pre-existing missing-V-suffix naming violation; cleanup script V1.1 target path reference is stale but non-destructive (fix-forward to V1.2 on next operator run); legacy dashboard filenames doc-librarian-dashboard-* retained until Phase D supersession as documented in plan V1.1 cross-references table. Totals: 39 -> 40 documents, 11 -> 12 superseded, active stays 28. Follow-up (2026-04-11, Session 27): buildout plan V1.1 -> V1.2 bump — reconciled spec with actual Phase A deliverables. Phase A overdelivered: pip-installable Python package v0.1.0 with 4 CLI subcommands and 36 pytest cases instead of planned file-only scaffold with YAML schemas. PRISM wrapper is 110-line CLI delegation wrapper instead of ~50-line pointer. Deferred items (schemas, examples, architecture doc) moved to Phase B. Phases B-D updated: standalone scripts replaced by integrated CLI subcommands. Phase A marked complete with commit hashes. Added Cowork sandbox limitation and tracked_dirs gap to risks. V1.1 marked superseded. Totals: 40 -> 41 documents, 12 -> 13 superseded, active stays 28.
registry_meta.last_audit_note: Fourth-pass OODA action on 2026-04-11 executed I1: cross-reference cascade from the Architectural Evolution Addendum V1.0. Six target documents received inline pointer notes at 13 total sections, each version-bumped and committed individually: project_prism_technical_architecture-v1_1 -> v1_2 (6 notes, 2c85d82); project_prism_project_plan-v2_1 -> v2_2 (3 notes, 4c8de50); project_prism_infrastructure-v1_1 -> v1_2 (2 notes, 3c2a8fe); project_prism_strategic_plan-v1_1 -> v1_2 (1 note, 8421f1d); prism-scientific-foundations-20260410-V1.0 -> 20260411-V1.1 (1 note, e5862ca); ETHICS_AND_CONSTRAINTS-v1_1 -> v1_2 (1 note, 2f62c90). Addendum registry entry updated: supplements list points to new versioned filenames; all 6 cross_references marked status: resolved with resolved_date 2026-04-11 and resolution strings citing the bump commits. Legacy superseded_by chains repointed to current active v1.2/v2.2 filenames since the intermediate v1_1/v2_1 artifacts were overwritten in place. pending_cross_reference_updates 6 -> 0. Prior-pass actions completed earlier on 2026-04-11: I4 (scientific foundations/NDA activation and ethics archive), I2 (a732f1a), I3 (bb60851), C1 (81ee593), C2 (329d823).

registry_meta.version_convention_note: Legacy files use VX_Y. New files use VX.Y. Files transition to dots on next version bump. Claude Chat project knowledge sanitizes dots to underscores in filenames on upload (V1.0 becomes V1_0). This is cosmetic — Chat copies and disk copies are the same logical document.
